name: Brenden Bishop
title: Builder & Technologist
intro: Husband, father, and technologist. Software Engineering leader and chronic entrepreneur. I love building, tinkering, and learning new things. Addicted to coffee and coding.
bio:
I build products and care a lot about how they get built.

Over the past decade, I've worked across engineering, product, and systems — building client-facing platforms, scaling teams, and launching my own products from 0→1.

More recently, I've been focused on how AI changes that process. Not just using it, but defining how it fits into real development workflows. I've been building internal tools, designing agentic systems, and shaping how teams use LLMs across the SDLC.

A lot of existing dev workflows are heavier than they need to be. Too much process, not enough iteration. I'm interested in stripping that down and making it easier to go from idea to working software.

Outside of my role at Morgan Stanley, I've built products like Forwheel and Rbitrage Capital. I still spend most of my time building, experimenting, and trying to get faster.
